Identification.LongTitle: An Act to amend the Youth Criminal Justice Act
Identification.RunningHead: An Act to amend the Youth Criminal Justice Act

Stages.StageText: Reprinted as amended by the Standing Committee on Justice and Human Rights as a working copy for the use of the House of Commons at Report Stage and as reported to the House on June 1, 2026

Provision.Text: This enactment amends the Youth Criminal Justice Act in order to

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: clarify the measures governing problematic substance use treatment programs for young persons;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: enable, in some cases, the youth justice court that finds a young person guilty of an offence to delay sentencing to enable the young person to participate in a problematic substance use treatment program or any other treatment program;

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: enable the youth justice court to include in certain orders the condition of attending a problematic substance use treatment program or any other treatment program; and

Provision.Label: (d)
Provision.Text: provide that the youth justice court may include in an order for conditional supervision a condition that the young person attend a problematic substance use treatment program or any other treatment program.
His Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: Section 6 of the Youth Criminal Justice Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1):

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of subsection (1), if the young person is alleged to have committed an offence under subsection 4(1) of the Controlled Drugs and Substances Act or subsection 8(1) of the Cannabis Act, the police officer shall consider whether it would be sufficient to refer the young person, with the young personâs consent, to a problematic substance use treatment program.

Section.Text: Section 10 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (3):

Subsection.Text: An extrajudicial sanction may include requiring a young person to attend a problematic substance use treatment program or any other treatment program.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 41:

If a youth justice court finds a young person guilty of any offence, the court may, before imposing a specific sentence, while taking into account the principles set out in subparagraphs 3(1)(b)(iv) and (v) and the purpose and principles set out in section 38 and with the consent of the Attorney General and the young person, delay sentencing to enable the young person to attend a problematic substance use treatment program or any other treatment program.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 42(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

A youth justice court shall, before imposing a youth sentence, consider any recommendations submitted under section 41, any pre-sentence report, any representations made by the parties to the proceedings or their counsel or agents and by the parents of the young person, any participation by the young person in a program referred to in section 41.1, and any other relevant information before the court.
Paragraph 42(2)(m) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (m)
Paragraph.Text: subject to subsection (3) (agreement of provincial director) and section 54, order the young person to attend a non-residential program â including a problematic substance use treatment program or any other treatment program â approved by the provincial director, at the times and on the terms that the court may fix, for a maximum of two hundred and forty hours, over a period not exceeding six months;

Section.Text: Subsection 55(2) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (e):

Paragraph.Label: (e.1)
Paragraph.Text: attend a problematic substance use treatment program or any other treatment program;

Section.Text: Subsection 105(3) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (c):

Paragraph.Label: (c.1)
Paragraph.Text: attend a problematic substance use treatment program or any other treatment program;

Section.Text: If Bill C-16, introduced in the 1st session of the 45th Parliament and entitled the Protecting Victims Act, receives royal assent, then on the first day on which both section 123 of that Act and section 1 of this Act are in force, section 6.1 of the Youth Criminal Justice Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1):

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of subsection (1), if the young person is alleged to have committed an offence under subsection 4(1) of the Controlled Drugs and Substances Act or subsection 8(1) of the Cannabis Act, the prosecutor shall consider whether it would be sufficient to refer the young person, with the young personâs consent, to a problematic substance use treatment program.

Section.Text: This Act, other than section 5.2, comes into force on the 30th day after the day on which it receives royal assent.
